mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, rising labor slack signals weakening consumer spending power, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contractions in real GDP and housing starts indicate a synchronized demand shock that depresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize during recovery phases, or the signal reverses to negative directionality in more than 60 percent of recovery period observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, restrictive monetary policy tightens financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor market deterioration and underemployment, which signals negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in monthly return models.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real personal consumption expenditures directly boost corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS growth remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or quarterly forward returns are not significantly positive during consumption expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle contraction phases signals impending earnings downgrades and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize during contraction phases, or the signal produces positive returns in more than 55 percent of late-cycle observations.
